channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says US shouldn't intervene in Syria as opposition forces gain ground
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that the United States should avoid engaging militarily in Syria amid an opposition offensive that has reached the capital's suburbs, declaring in a social media post, "THIS IS NOT OUR FIGHT.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump isn't back in office but he's already pushing his agenda and negotiating with world leaders
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — He's making threats, traveling abroad and negotiating with world leaders. Donald Trump has more than a month and a half to go before he's sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Speaker’s Lobby: Thermonuclear Fracas
item.description: Despite the intent to find solutions following the assassination attempt of President-elect Trump back in July, bipartisan lawmakers got into a heated exchange.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Election Day has long passed. In some states, legislatures are working to undermine the results
item.description: Voters in some parts of the country are discovering that having their say at the ballot box is not necessarily the final word, even though this year's election was over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The DNC chair candidates discuss Joe Biden, Kamala Harris and winning Latino voters
item.description: Four Democrats are running for Democratic National Committee chair, looking to take on the task of reinvigorating a party demoralized by a second loss to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bureau of Prisons agrees to court monitor, public acknowledgement of staff-on-inmate sexual abuse
item.description: A legal settlement in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse will force the federal Bureau of Prisons to open its doors to a court-appointed monitor and publicly acknowledge pervasive misconduct at its now-shuttered women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Michael Balsamo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pete Hegseth says he had ‘substantive conversation’ with Joni Ernst as Trump signals support
item.description: President-elect Trump threw his support behind embattled defense secretary pick Pete Hegseth Friday as the nominee continues to meet with Republican senators.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps forceful ally of hard-line immigration policies to head Customs and Border Protection
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The picture of who will be in charge of executing President-elect Donald Trump's hard-line immigration and border policies has come into sharper focus after he announced his picks to head Customs and Border Protection and also the agency tasked with deporting immigrants in the coun
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Military leaders are rattled by a list of 'woke' officers that a group urges Hegseth to fire
item.description: The list compiled by the American Accountability Foundation includes 20 general officers or senior admirals and a disproportionate number of female officers.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how Trump plans to install longtime ally Kash Patel as FBI director
item.description: If FBI Director Christopher Wray does not voluntarily vacate his position, Donald Trump will be the only president in U.S. history to have fired and installed two separate FBI directors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden weighs preemptive pardons before leaving office
item.description: President Joe Biden is weighing sweeping pardons for allies, who the White House believes could be targeted by President-elect Donald Trump’s incoming administration, according to people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden is considering preemptive pardons for officials and allies before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is weighing whether to issue sweeping pardons for officials and allies who the White House fears could be unjustly targeted by President-elect Donald Trump’s administration, a preemptive move that would be a novel and risky use of the president’s extraordinary c
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Conservatives worry Congress won't have 'spine' for spending overhaul after DOGE meetings
item.description: Some Republicans are questioning whether the goals set out for the Department of Government Efficiency will translate to significant change.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AOC launches bid for top Democratic role on House Oversight Committee
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., announced she is running for the top Democratic position on the House Oversight Committee next Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Boston City Council member is arrested on fraud and theft charges
item.description: Boston city councilor Tania Fernandes Anderson was arrested Friday on federal fraud charges, accused of funneling part of an inflated bonus payment to a staffer to whom she was related into her own pockets during an exchange at a City Hall bathroom, the U.S. attorney's office said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump's position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg's
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC formed less than a month before the election that focused on advertising intended to convince voters that Donald Trump 's stance on abortion was akin to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shouting match erupts over 9/11 during Secret Service hearing
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri abortion ban slated to lift Friday if judge allows
item.description: A judge must decide whether “targeted regulation of abortion providers” laws are also unconstitutional under Amendment 3.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin McCarthy: Gaetz lied to Trump before AG nomination
item.description: Should the House Ethics Committee still release its report on Gaetz, even if he’s not going to be part of Trump’s Cabinet?
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: California lawmakers kick off special session to 'Trump-proof' the Golden State
item.description: California lawmakers on Monday are kicking off a special legislative session in what Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) has deemed efforts to "Trump-proof" the Golden State from potential federal interference.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Nadler bows out of Judiciary race, will endorse Raskin
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) is ceding his position as top Democrat on the powerful Judiciary Committee amid a tough challenge from Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.), — a stunning shift on a powerful committee that’s sure to play a considerable role in the oversight of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Trump criticizes Chronister after sheriff's withdrawal, days after praising him
item.description: President Donald Trump challenged reports of Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister's DEA withdrawal, saying he didn't want the sheriff days his cabinet days after nominating him.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump selects Jared Isaacman to lead NASA
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced he picked Jared Isaacman to lead NASA in his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Majority of Supreme Court seems reluctant to block state bans on medical treatments for minors
item.description: Hearing a high-profile culture-war clash, a majority of the Supreme Court seemed reluctant Wednesday to block Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump reportedly considering DeSantis as replacement nominee for defense secretary
item.description: Several reports, including from the Wall Street Journal, have said Governor Ron DeSantis could become President-elect Trump's new defense secretary nominee. The news comes amid a growing number of allegations against Trump's original nominee Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota House panel votes to absorb legal fees of DFLer in employment dispute
item.description: A DFL state lawmaker will have legal fees covered by the Minnesota House after alleging her rights were violated by an outside employer. Republicans say the move sets a bad precedent.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
